title: Bind Grabowski operator events to the actual target repository
goal: Correct Grabowski's Chronik producer so agent-run events identify the actual task target repository and bounded operation without leaking argv, secrets or private paths, while retaining current event-id and trust-tier semantics.

acceptance.assertion: For repository-bound tasks, subject.repo is derived from canonical task metadata and names the actual target repository rather than always heimgewebe/grabowski.
Tasks without a provable repository target use an explicit unknown or host-scoped subject and never fabricate a repository identity.
The event carries a typed bounded operation or task class sufficient for historical queries without copying raw argv, environment values, secrets or private path details.
Event IDs remain deterministic and unique across started, completed and blocked states, and existing outbox retry and deduplication behavior remains valid.

A real isolated Grabowski task targeting a non-Grabowski repository produces started and terminal events whose subject.repo matches that repository, and the merged Chronik importer preserves those fields.
Grabowski task, workspace, Chronik-outbox and security tests plus GitHub CI pass at the exact reviewed head.

rollback.trigger: The new derivation exposes private command data, misidentifies non-repository tasks or changes task execution semantics.
rollback.procedure: Restore the previous producer mapping, retain the direct Chronik importer, and mark target identity unknown until a narrower typed task field is available.

conditions: a foreign Grabowski worktree, branch, lease or process overlaps the producer files
the target repository cannot be derived from canonical task state without parsing raw shell text
the change would make Chronik authoritative for current task or repository truth
privacy, determinism, replay or deduplication tests fail

live_finding.finding: Existing Grabowski outbox events preserve lifecycle truth but do not reliably identify the repository actually worked on.

does_not_establish: permission to inspect or persist raw argv or secrets
absence of concurrent Grabowski work without a fresh preflight
metadata.outcome: Grabowski now emits repository-scoped lifecycle events for the actual canonical task target, with explicit bounded operation and task class. The merged runtime was deployed and a real non-Grabowski HausKI task was imported into Chronik with deterministic idempotent readback.

validation.chronik_scoped_query: PASS: one observed completed event for heimgewebe/hausKI, implement, coding

findings_fixed: Repository identity was previously hard-coded to heimgewebe/grabowski.
Operation semantics initially allowed an uninformative other value without an explicit task field.
The runbook demo depended on a stale checkout and was corrected to the installed runtime contract.
review.separate_follow_up: CHRONIK-ECOSYSTEM-CLOSEOUT-V1-T007 records the nonblocking producer-component query-filter defect discovered during live readback.

acceptance_evidence.target-repo-derived-from-task-record: Task 59e70b25df4c4b37872ddca1 used only the canonical repo:/home/alex/repos/hausKI claim and emitted subject.repo=heimgewebe/hausKI.
acceptance_evidence.non-repository-task-boundary: Grabowski tests cover explicit host scope when no single canonical repository target is provable; no argv inference is used.
acceptance_evidence.bounded-operation-semantics: The live events contain operation=implement and task_class=coding while omitting argv, cwd, environment, secrets and private path data.
acceptance_evidence.identity-and-dedup-stable: Started and completed have distinct deterministic event IDs; a repeated Chronik import added 0 and recognized both existing events.
acceptance_evidence.forward-proof: The real HausKI task completed successfully; Chronik imported both lifecycle events and returned the observed completed event through repo, operation, task-class and outcome filters.
acceptance_evidence.full-regression-proof: 151 focused and 1596 full Grabowski tests passed at the exact reviewed head; GitHub CI and the merged runtime deploy passed.
